questions.question: An electron is placed in an electric field where the field lines point from left to right. In which direction will the electron experience a force?
options: To the left
To the right
Upwards
It will experience no force
questions.explanation:
Field lines show the direction of force on a positive charge. Since an electron is negative, it experiences a force in the opposite direction to the field lines.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which diagram correctly describes the electric field between two oppositely charged parallel conducting plates?
options: Parallel, equally spaced lines pointing from the positive to the negative plate
Parallel, equally spaced lines pointing from the negative to the positive plate
Circular lines originating from the centre of the plates
Lines that bend away from the centre gap between the plates
questions.explanation:
The field between oppositely charged parallel plates is uniform, represented by parallel, equally spaced lines pointing from positive to negative.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: If the spacing between electric field lines in a certain region increases, what happens to the force experienced by a charge moved into that region?
options: The force decreases
The force increases
The force remains constant
The force changes direction
questions.explanation:
Increased spacing between field lines indicates a weaker electric field. Therefore, a charge in that region will experience a smaller force.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A positive charge and a negative charge are placed near each other. How do the field lines between them behave?
options: They curve from the positive charge to the negative charge
They curve from the negative charge to the positive charge
They point straight outwards from both charges and never meet
They form concentric circles around both charges
questions.explanation:
Field lines go away from positive and towards negative. In a pair of unlike charges, the lines curve from the positive charge to the negative one.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which of the following would experience the greatest force in a uniform electric field?
options: A particle with a charge of $+2\text{ C}$
A particle with a charge of $+1\text{ C}$
A neutral atom
A particle with a charge of $-1\text{ C}$
questions.explanation:
The force in an electric field depends on the magnitude of the charge. A charge of $2\text{ C}$ has the highest magnitude among the choices and will experience the most force.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
